title: Employee Prisma Schema Models
description: Create Prisma schema models for Employee and EmployeeID. Employee mirrors SIS data (sisEmployeeId, fullName, jobTitle, email, status). EmployeeID stores local metadata (photoUrl, expirationDate, createdAt, updatedAt).
tests: Schema file validates with Prisma
Migration creates tables correctly
Relations between Employee and EmployeeID are properly defined
Required fields have appropriate constraints

title: FACTS SIS Employee Sync Service
description: Create a service module to fetch employee data from FACTS SIS REST API. Includes authentication, error handling, and data transformation to match Employee schema.
tests: Service successfully authenticates with FACTS API
Service fetches employee list and transforms data correctly
Service handles API errors gracefully
Service validates required fields from SIS response

title: Employee Sync Background Job
description: Implement a scheduled background job (daily/hourly) that syncs employee data from FACTS SIS to local database. Updates existing employees and creates new ones based on SIS status.
tests: Job successfully runs on schedule
New employees from SIS are created in database
Existing employees are updated with latest SIS data
Inactive employees have status updated correctly
Job handles failures without crashing

title: Admin Employee List View
description: Create an admin route that displays a list of all employees with their status, expiration dates, and photo status. Includes search/filter capabilities.
tests: Admin can view list of all employees
List displays employee name, job title, status, and expiration date
Search filters employees by name or email
Filter by status (active/inactive) works correctly
Non-admin users cannot access this route

title: Employee ID Route (Own ID View)
description: Create a route for employees to view their own ID. Displays employee information and provides download button. Employees can only access their own ID.
tests: Employee can view their own ID page
Employee cannot view other employees' IDs
Page displays employee name, title, photo, and expiration date
Download button is visible and functional
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login

title: Admin Photo Upload
description: Implement photo upload functionality for admins. Admins can upload/replace employee photos. Photos are stored in file storage and URLs saved to EmployeeID record.
tests: Admin can upload photo for any employee
Uploaded photo replaces existing photo if present
Photo is validated (format, size)
Photo URL is saved to EmployeeID record
Non-admin users cannot upload photos
Error handling for invalid files works correctly

title: Expiration Date Management
description: Implement expiration date management. Default expiration is July 1 of current school year. Admins can view and override expiration dates for any employee.
tests: Default expiration date is set to July 1 of current school year
Admin can view expiration date for any employee
Admin can update expiration date
Expiration date is stored correctly in database
Date validation prevents invalid dates
Employees can see their own expiration date

title: QR Code Generation Service
description: Create a service that generates QR codes for employee IDs. QR code links to public verification URL: /verify/:employeeId. Service handles error cases and returns base64 or buffer.
tests: Service generates valid QR code
QR code contains correct verification URL format
QR code is readable and scans correctly
Service handles invalid employee IDs gracefully
QR code size and format are appropriate for PDF embedding

title: PDF ID Card Generation
description: Implement PDF generation for employee IDs using @react-pdf/renderer. ID includes front (name, title, photo, expiration, logo) and back (QR code). Wallet-size format with proper branding.
tests: PDF generates successfully for valid employee
PDF includes all required fields (name, title, photo, expiration, logo)
PDF includes QR code on back
PDF is wallet-sized and printable
School branding (logo, colors) is correctly applied
Error handling for missing photo or data works correctly

title: PDF Download Endpoint
description: Create server endpoint/loader that generates and streams PDF ID card for download. Employees can download their own PDF, admins can download any employee's PDF.
tests: Employee can download their own PDF ID
Employee cannot download other employees' PDFs
Admin can download any employee's PDF
PDF is generated and streamed correctly
Content-Type header is set to application/pdf
Download works with valid authentication

title: Public Verification Route
description: Create public (unauthenticated) route at /verify/:employeeId that displays employee verification status. Shows name, title, status (active/inactive), expiration, and validity badge.
tests: Verification page is publicly accessible without authentication
Page displays employee name and job title
Page shows active/inactive status correctly
Page displays expiration date
Page shows valid/invalid badge based on status and expiration
Invalid employee IDs show appropriate error message
Page includes school branding

title: Verification Status Logic
description: Implement business logic to determine if an ID is valid: active status from SIS AND current date <= expiration date. Invalid otherwise. Logic used by verification page.
tests: Valid status returned for active employee with future expiration
Invalid status returned for inactive employee
Invalid status returned for expired ID (past expiration date)
Invalid status returned for employee missing from SIS
Status calculation uses current date correctly

title: Google OAuth Integration for Employees
description: Integrate Google OAuth authentication with school email domain restriction. Only users with school email domain can authenticate. On first login, create Employee record if user exists in SIS.
tests: Google OAuth authentication works correctly
Only school email domain users can authenticate
Non-school email users are rejected
First-time employee login creates Employee record if in SIS
Returning employees can log in successfully
Session is created and maintained correctly

title: Admin Role Management
description: Implement admin role assignment system. Admins are identified by a flag in User model or separate Admin model. Admin permissions enforced across all admin routes.
tests: Admin users can access admin routes
Non-admin users are denied access to admin routes
Admin flag/role is stored and retrieved correctly
Permission checks work in route loaders/actions
Admin users can perform admin actions (upload photos, manage IDs)

title: Employee ID Creation on First View
description: When an employee first views their ID (or admin views employee ID), automatically create EmployeeID record if it doesn't exist. Set default expiration date (July 1) if not set.
tests: EmployeeID record created when employee first views their ID
Default expiration date set to July 1 if not provided
Record creation happens automatically without admin intervention
Subsequent views don't recreate the record
Admin viewing employee ID triggers creation if missing

title: Photo Storage Integration
description: Implement file storage integration for employee photos. Use Fly.io volume or S3-compatible storage. Store photos with unique keys and generate URLs for database storage.
tests: Photos are uploaded to configured storage backend
Photos are stored with unique, secure keys
Photo URLs are generated and stored in database
Photos can be retrieved by URL
Storage handles errors gracefully (full disk, permissions, etc.)

title: Admin Employee Detail View
description: Create admin route to view individual employee details including SIS sync status, photo, expiration date, and actions (upload photo, update expiration, view/download ID).
tests: Admin can view employee detail page
Page displays all employee information
Page shows photo upload interface
Page shows expiration date editing
Page includes download/view ID button
Non-admin users cannot access employee detail pages

title: School Branding Configuration
description: Create configuration system for school branding (logo URL, colors, name). Branding applied to PDF IDs and verification pages. Configuration stored in environment variables or database.
tests: School logo is displayed on PDF IDs
School colors are applied correctly
School name appears on ID and verification pages
Branding configuration can be updated
Default branding is used if not configured

title: Verification Page SEO and Metadata
description: Add proper SEO metadata to verification page including title, description, and Open Graph tags. Ensures verification pages are shareable and indexable appropriately.
tests: Verification page has appropriate title tag
Page includes meta description
Open Graph tags are present and correct
Page title includes employee name and status
Metadata updates based on verification status

title: Error Handling and User Feedback
description: Implement comprehensive error handling for ID generation, photo uploads, and verification. Display user-friendly error messages. Log errors for debugging.
tests: Missing employee data shows appropriate error message
Photo upload errors are displayed to user
PDF generation errors are handled gracefully
Verification errors show clear messages
Errors are logged for debugging
Users see helpful feedback messages for all actions

title: Rate Limiting on Verification Endpoint
description: Implement rate limiting on public verification endpoint to prevent abuse and scraping. Use express-rate-limit or similar. Allow reasonable access while blocking excessive requests.
tests: Verification endpoint enforces rate limits
Normal usage is not blocked by rate limits
Excessive requests are rate-limited correctly
Rate limit errors return appropriate status codes
Rate limits are configurable
Rate limiting doesn't affect authenticated routes

title: Employee ID Layout Component
description: Create reusable React component for rendering ID card layout (front and back). Component used for both PDF generation and preview. Includes all fields and proper styling.
tests: Component renders ID front with all required fields
Component renders ID back with QR code
Component handles missing photo gracefully
Component applies school branding correctly
Component is responsive and printable
Component validates required props

title: SIS Sync Status Dashboard
description: Create admin dashboard showing SIS sync status: last sync time, sync errors, employees pending sync, sync statistics. Helps admins monitor data freshness.
tests: Dashboard displays last sync timestamp
Dashboard shows sync errors if any occurred
Dashboard lists employees with sync issues
Dashboard shows sync statistics (total, active, inactive)
Dashboard is only accessible to admins
Dashboard refreshes sync status on demand

title: Employee ID Expiration Notifications
description: Implement system to identify and notify admins of upcoming ID expirations (e.g., within 30 days). Display warnings in admin interface and optionally send email alerts.
tests: System identifies IDs expiring within 30 days
Expiring IDs are displayed in admin interface
Warning messages are clear and actionable
Expiration calculation uses correct date logic
Already expired IDs are identified separately
Email notifications can be sent (optional)

title: Integration Tests for Full ID Workflow
description: Create end-to-end integration tests covering complete ID workflow: SIS sync → employee login → view ID → admin uploads photo → generate PDF → verify via QR code.
tests: E2E test: Employee can complete full ID workflow
E2E test: Admin can manage employee ID end-to-end
E2E test: Public verification works from QR code scan
E2E test: Expiration logic works in real scenarios
All integration tests pass reliably
Tests cover error scenarios as well as happy paths

title: FACTS Profile Picture Integration with Caching
description: When displaying employee photos, if no uploaded photo exists, fetch the profile picture from the FACTS API endpoint /People/{personId}/ProfilePicture (using sisEmployeeId as personId). Cache the fetched photo in local storage and update the EmployeeID.photoUrl field. If the FACTS API call fails, show a "No Photo" placeholder. Uploaded photos always take precedence over FACTS photos.
tests: Unit: Service function fetches profile picture from FACTS API using correct personId
Unit: Service function handles FACTS API errors (404, 500, network) gracefully and returns null
Unit: Service function caches fetched FACTS photo to storage and updates EmployeeID record
Unit: Photo fetching logic prioritizes uploaded photo over FACTS photo
E2E: Employee ID displays FACTS photo when no uploaded photo exists
E2E: Employee ID displays uploaded photo when both uploaded and FACTS photos exist
E2E: Employee ID shows "No Photo" placeholder when FACTS API returns 404 or error
Manual: Verify FACTS photos are cached and don't require repeated API calls

title: Landing Page with Embedded Login Form and Reimagined Navigation
description: Replace the current marketing landing page with a simple landing page that includes brief information about the JPG ID System and an embedded login form (Google OAuth). Reimagine the header and footer navigation: remove the search bar and theme switcher, add navigation links for authenticated users (My ID, Admin Dashboard if admin), and update branding to reflect the JPG ID System.
tests: Unit: Landing page displays brief system information and embedded login form
Unit: Header navigation shows "My ID" link for authenticated employees
Unit: Header navigation shows "Admin Dashboard" link for authenticated admin users
Unit: Header navigation hides search bar
Unit: Footer does not display theme switcher
E2E: Unauthenticated user can view landing page with login form
E2E: Authenticated employee can navigate to "My ID" from header
E2E: Authenticated admin can navigate to "Admin Dashboard" from header
Manual: Verify landing page design is clean and professional for internal tool use

title: Add to Wallet (Apple Wallet / Google Pay)
description: Add an "Add to Wallet" button on the employee's own ID page (/employee/id) that auto-detects the device (iOS/Android) and generates the appropriate wallet pass file. The pass mirrors the PDF ID card, including employee name, job title, photo, expiration date, QR code for verification, and school branding. On iOS, it generates an Apple Wallet pass (.pkpass); on Android, it generates a Google Pay pass. The button downloads the pass file, which users can then add to their device's wallet app.
tests: Unit: Service function generates valid Apple Wallet pass (.pkpass) with all required fields (name, title, photo, expiration, QR code, branding)
Unit: Service function generates valid Google Pay pass with all required fields
Unit: Pass generation handles missing photo gracefully (shows placeholder or omits photo)
Unit: Pass generation handles missing branding gracefully (uses defaults)
Unit: Device detection correctly identifies iOS vs Android from user agent
E2E: "Add to Wallet" button appears on employee ID page for authenticated employees
E2E: Clicking button on iOS device downloads .pkpass file
E2E: Clicking button on Android device downloads Google Pay pass file
E2E: Button is not visible for unauthenticated users
Manual: Verify generated Apple Wallet pass can be successfully added to Apple Wallet app on iOS device
Manual: Verify generated Google Pay pass can be successfully added to Google Wallet app on Android device
Manual: Verify pass displays correctly in wallet apps with all information (photo, QR code, etc.)

title: Student Prisma Schema Models
description: Create Prisma schema models for Student and StudentID. Student mirrors SIS data (sisStudentId, fullName, email, status) with an additional editable fullName field that admins can modify. StudentID stores local metadata (photoUrl, expirationDate, createdAt, updatedAt).
tests: Schema file validates with Prisma
Migration creates tables correctly
Relations between Student and StudentID are properly defined
Required fields have appropriate constraints

title: FACTS SIS Student Sync Service
description: Create a service module to fetch student data from FACTS SIS REST API /Students endpoint. Includes authentication, error handling, and data transformation to match Student schema.
tests: Service successfully authenticates with FACTS API
Service fetches student list and transforms data correctly
Service handles API errors gracefully
Service validates required fields from SIS response

title: Student Sync Background Job
description: Extend the scheduled background job to also sync student data from FACTS SIS to local database. Creates new students, updates existing students with latest SIS data, but preserves admin-edited names.
tests: Job successfully syncs students on schedule
New students from SIS are created in database
Existing students are updated with latest SIS data
Admin-edited names are preserved during sync
Inactive students have status updated correctly
Job handles failures without crashing

title: Admin Student List View
description: Create a separate admin route at /admin/students that displays a list of all students with their status, expiration dates, and photo status. Includes search/filter capabilities.
tests: Admin can view list of all students
List displays student name, status, and expiration date
Search filters students by name or email
Filter by status (active/inactive) works correctly
Non-admin users cannot access this route

title: Admin Student Detail View with Name Editing
description: Create admin route at /admin/students/$studentId to view individual student details including photo, expiration date, and actions. Includes ability for admins to edit and save student name (unlike employees which are read-only from SIS).
tests: Admin can view student detail page
Page displays all student information
Admin can edit student name and save changes
Edited name persists across SIS syncs
Page shows photo upload interface
Page shows expiration date editing
Page includes download/view ID button
Non-admin users cannot access student detail pages

title: Admin Photo Upload for Students
description: Implement photo upload functionality for students (similar to employees). Admins can upload/replace student photos. Photos are stored in file storage and URLs saved to StudentID record.
tests: Admin can upload photo for any student
Uploaded photo replaces existing photo if present
Photo is validated (format, size)
Photo URL is saved to StudentID record
Non-admin users cannot upload photos
Error handling for invalid files works correctly

title: Expiration Date Management for Students
description: Implement expiration date management for students. Default expiration is July 1 of current school year (same as employees). Admins can view and override expiration dates for any student.
tests: Default expiration date is set to July 1 of current school year
Admin can view expiration date for any student
Admin can update expiration date
Expiration date is stored correctly in database
Date validation prevents invalid dates
Students can see their own expiration date

title: Student ID Route (Own ID View)
description: Create a route at /student/id for students to view their own ID. Displays student information with "STUDENT" label instead of job title. Provides download button. Students can only access their own ID.
tests: Student can view their own ID page
Student cannot view other students' IDs
Page displays student name, "STUDENT" label, photo, and expiration date
Download button is visible and functional
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login

title: Student PDF Download Endpoint
description: Create server endpoint/loader that generates and streams PDF ID card for student download. Students can download their own PDF, admins can download any student's PDF. PDF shows "STUDENT" instead of job title.
tests: Student can download their own PDF ID
Student cannot download other students' PDFs
Admin can download any student's PDF
PDF is generated and streamed correctly
PDF displays "STUDENT" label instead of job title
Content-Type header is set to application/pdf
Download works with valid authentication

title: Google OAuth Integration for Students
description: Extend Google OAuth authentication to recognize students. Students with @jpgacademy.org email addresses can authenticate. On first login, create Student record if user exists in SIS.
tests: Students can authenticate with Google OAuth using @jpgacademy.org email
First-time student login creates Student record if in SIS
Returning students can log in successfully
Session is created and maintained correctly
Students are distinguished from employees in the system

title: Update ID Card Layout for Person Type Display
description: Update the ID card component to display "FACULTY" for employees and "STUDENT" for students instead of using job title. This ensures consistent labeling across all ID cards while maintaining the academic year display.
tests: Employee ID cards display "FACULTY" label
Student ID cards display "STUDENT" label
Academic year displays correctly for both types
PDF generation works for both employees and students
Preview components work for both types
Branding is applied consistently

title: Public Verification Route for Students
description: Extend the public verification route at /verify/:id to support both employees and students. Route auto-detects person type and displays appropriate verification information with "Student" or "Faculty" label.
tests: Verification page works for both employees and students
Page displays correct person type label (Student/Faculty)
Page shows active/inactive status correctly for students
Page displays expiration date for students
Page shows valid/invalid badge based on status and expiration
Invalid IDs show appropriate error message

title: Student ID Expiration Notifications
description: Extend the expiration notification system to include student IDs. Display warnings in admin interface for students with expiring IDs (within 30 days).
tests: System identifies student IDs expiring within 30 days
Expiring student IDs are displayed in admin interface
Warning messages are clear and actionable
Already expired student IDs are identified separately
Notifications work for both employees and students

title: Differentiate Faculty vs Staff on Employee IDs
description: Update the ID card component to display FACULTY or STAFF for employees based on job title. Employees with job titles containing 'Faculty' should display FACULTY badge. Employees with job titles containing 'Staff' (but not 'Faculty') should display STAFF badge. Default to STAFF if neither match. This ensures accurate role-based identification across all employee ID cards.
tests: Employee with job title containing 'Faculty' displays FACULTY badge
Employee with job title containing 'Staff' (but not 'Faculty') displays STAFF badge
Employee with job title containing neither displays STAFF badge (default)
PDF generation includes correct person type for both faculty and staff
Preview components display correct badge
Branding is applied consistently

title: Gmail Signature Viewing for Admins
description: Display Gmail signatures on admin employee detail page using Google Workspace domain-wide delegation. Signatures are fetched via service account impersonation and cached in the database with automatic refresh (> 7 days old).
tests: Service fetches signature via service account impersonation
Signature is cached to database
Signature is displayed on admin employee detail page
Background refresh triggers when cache is stale (> 7 days)
Background refresh triggers when signature is missing
Graceful handling when service account not configured
Graceful handling when API call fails

title: Store & Display Department from FACTS SIS
description: Add a separate department field to the Employee model, pulled from FACTS SIS. Display department on admin employee list (filterable), admin employee detail page, and employee own page. Department is stored separately from jobTitle (which currently uses the department field).
tests: Department field pulled from FACTS and stored in Employee model
Department displayed on admin employee detail page in Personnel Record section
Department displayed as filterable column/filter on admin employee list
Department visible on employee own ID page info section
Sync preserves department across updates
Null/empty department handled gracefully with fallback

title: Unified Bulk ID Card Print with Criteria Selection
description: Create a unified admin route at /admin/print for bulk ID card printing. Supports both employees and students. Admin can filter by people type (employees/students/both), grade level (students), faculty/staff classification (employees), department, and active/inactive status. Supports select-all or individual cherry-picking. Generates multi-card-per-page PDF with front/back.
tests: Admin can access /admin/print route
Admin can filter employees by department and faculty/staff type
Admin can filter students by grade level
Admin can select mixed employees and students
Generated PDF contains correct cards for all selected people
Front/back pages render correctly for mixed employee and student cards
Non-admin users cannot access print route

title: Gmail Signature Template CRUD
description: Create SignatureTemplate Prisma model (name, htmlContent, isDefault, createdAt, updatedAt). Build admin UI at /admin/signatures/templates for creating, editing, and deleting HTML signature templates. Templates support placeholders: {{fullName}}, {{firstName}}, {{lastName}}, {{jobTitle}}, {{department}}, {{email}}, {{phone}}, {{schoolName}}. Includes live preview with sample data.
tests: SignatureTemplate model created with correct fields and constraints
Admin can create a new signature template
Admin can edit an existing signature template
Admin can delete a signature template
Template placeholders are listed and documented in the UI
Live preview renders template with sample employee data
Non-admin users cannot access template management

title: Gmail Signature Preview & Push
description: Admin UI at /admin/signatures/push to select a template, select recipients (all employees, or filter by department/status/individual), preview rendered signatures per person, and push to Gmail. Uses Google Workspace domain-wide delegation with gmail.settings.sharing scope to SET signatures via PUT /users/{email}/settings/sendAs/{email}.
tests: Admin can select a template and see recipient list
Admin can filter recipients by department, status, or individual selection
Preview shows correctly rendered signature for each selected employee
Push action updates Gmail signature for selected employees
Gmail API called with correct scope (gmail.settings.sharing) and PUT method
Partial failures handled gracefully (some succeed, some fail)
Non-admin users cannot access signature push

title: Signature Push History & Status Tracking
description: Track signature push history: which employees had signatures pushed, when, which template was used, and success/failure status. Display push status on admin employee detail page. Show bulk push results summary after each push operation. New SignaturePushLog model.
tests: SignaturePushLog model created with correct fields
Push operations create log entries for each employee
Admin employee detail page shows last signature push status
Bulk push results summary displayed after push operation
Push history is queryable by employee, template, or date
Failed pushes are logged with error details
